inlineContent.text: An array specifically designed to hold
. Packs data tightly, so it saves memory for large array sizes.
Differences between packed arrays, typed arrays, and untyped arrays:
Packed arrays are generally faster to iterate on and modify compared to a typed array of the same type (e.g.

inlineContent.code: Vector2```). Also, packed arrays consume less memory. As a downside, packed arrays are less flexible as they don't offer as many convenience methods such as

inlineContent.text: Array/map(method:)``. Typed arrays are in turn faster to iterate on and modify than untyped arrays.
Packed arrays are always passed by reference. To get a copy of an array that can be modified independently of the original array, use
the case for built-in properties and methods. The returned packed array of these are a copies, and changing it will
affect the original value. To update a built-in property you need to modify the returned array, and then assign it to the property again.
